summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for the File Explorer status bar. Microsoft Learn describes showStatusBar as a File Explorer Classic advanced setting, the 25H2 raw registry dump and 25H2 default-user hive both corroborate the ShowStatusBar value family, the app now writes the same current-user value through VisibilityTweakProvider, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowStatusBar with both Data: 0 and Data: 1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Show Status Bar
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer can show or hide its status bar at the bottom of the window.
setting.why_people_change_it: People show the status bar when they want extra file and selection details visible, or hide it when they want a cleaner Explorer window.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: This is a low-risk Explorer preference. Enabling it adds status details to Explorer windows, while disabling it keeps the interface cleaner.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Learn describes showStatusBar as a File Explorer Classic advanced setting. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowStatusBar under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch and in the siloed user branches, while the 25H2 default-user hive sets ShowStatusBar = 1. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing ShowStatusBar = 0 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing ShowStatusBar = 1 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1. The app now maps the same path and value through VisibilityTweakProvider, so the shipped apply surface matches the validated runtime preference.

allowed_values.label: Hide status bar
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer does not show the status bar. The Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart.

allowed_values.label: Show status bar
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer shows the status bar. The 25H2 default-user hive sets ShowStatusBar = 1, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart.

targets.notes: This record validates the live Explorer runtime surface. The Microsoft settings reference describes the feature, while the 25H2 dump/default hive and Procmon probe corroborate the direct registry value.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now exposes ShowStatusBar through VisibilityTweakProvider and writes the same HKCU Explorer\Advanced value that the runtime probe validated.

windows_defaults.label: 25H2 default-user baseline

states.rationale: HKCU25H2.reg sets ShowStatusBar = 1 in the 25H2 default-user hive.

windows_defaults.notes: The validated VM user baseline differed from the default-user hive, but the 25H2 default profile still corroborates the expected default state.

recommended_profiles.label: Show status bar
intended_for: Users who want extra details visible in Explorer windows
avoid_for: Users who prefer a cleaner Explorer interface

states.rationale: This matches the 25H2 default-user hive and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the status bar shown in Explorer.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Explorer to use less UI space.
tradeoffs: Adds status details to Explorer windows.
Uses a little more visual space.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide status bar
intended_for: Users who prefer a cleaner Explorer window
avoid_for: Users who rely on status details

states.rationale: This matches the runtime-observed Explorer read for the hidden state.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer to hide the status bar.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want visible file and selection details at the bottom of Explorer.
tradeoffs: Leaves the UI cleaner but with less status detail.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: settings-common File Explorer Classic advanced settings
evidence.summary: Microsoft Learn describes showStatusBar as a File Explorer Classic advanced setting and says it indicates whether File Explorer should show the status bar.

evidence.title: 25H2 raw registry and default-hive corroboration for ShowStatusBar
evidence.summary: The 25H2 raw registry dump lists ShowStatusBar under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch and the siloed user branches, and the 25H2 default-user hive sets ShowStatusBar = 1.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer ShowStatusBar runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote ShowStatusBar = 0 and ShowStatusBar = 1, restarted Explorer after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowStatusBar with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

evidence.title: Current app implementation for ShowStatusBar
evidence.summary: VisibilityTweakProvider now exposes explorer.show-status-bar and writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowStatusBar = 1 for the current user.

decision.why: The 25H2 dump and default-user hive corroborate the ShowStatusBar value family, Microsoft Learn describes showStatusBar as a File Explorer Classic advanced setting, the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both states, and the current app implementation now writes the same validated value. This closes the loop from dump lineage to runtime behavior to shipped app mapping.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowStatusBar ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowStatusBar ... Data: 1; RESTORED_EXISTS=True RESTORED_VALUE=0
validation_proof.notes: The guest-local result file was copied back to the host scratch area during validation. The VM user baseline was 0 and was restored after the probe.
